questions.question: Which two gases will diffuse at the same rate, at the same temperature? A carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide, B carbon monoxide and nitrogen, C chlorine and fluorine, D nitrogen and oxygen
questions.text: Which two gases will diffuse at the same rate, at the same temperature? A carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide, B carbon monoxide and nitrogen, C chlorine and fluorine, D nitrogen and oxygen
options: A carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide
B carbon monoxide and nitrogen
C chlorine and fluorine
D nitrogen and oxygen

questions.question: Particle P has an atomic number of 18, a mass number of 40 and no overall charge. Particle Q has an atomic number of 19, a mass number of 40 and a single positive charge. Which statement is correct? A They are isotopes of the same element., B They are both ions., C Q has more neutrons than P., D They have the same number of electrons in their outer shell.
questions.text: Particle P has an atomic number of 18, a mass number of 40 and no overall charge. Particle Q has an atomic number of 19, a mass number of 40 and a single positive charge. Which statement is correct? A They are isotopes of the same element., B They are both ions., C Q has more neutrons than P., D They have the same number of electrons in their outer shell.
options: A They are isotopes of the same element.
B They are both ions.
C Q has more neutrons than P.
D They have the same number of electrons in their outer shell.

questions.question: Which statement about the properties of metals is correct? A Metals are malleable because the layers of positive ions can slide over each other., B Metals  conduct  electricity  when  solid  because  the  positive  ions  move  freely  through  the metal., C Metals conduct electricity because there is a strong force of attraction between the positive ions and the delocalised electrons., D Metals have a high melting point because the positive ions attract each other strongly. 4
questions.text: Which statement about the properties of metals is correct? A Metals are malleable because the layers of positive ions can slide over each other., B Metals  conduct  electricity  when  solid  because  the  positive  ions  move  freely  through  the metal., C Metals conduct electricity because there is a strong force of attraction between the positive ions and the delocalised electrons., D Metals have a high melting point because the positive ions attract each other strongly. 4
options: A Metals are malleable because the layers of positive ions can slide over each other.
B Metals  conduct  electricity  when  solid  because  the  positive  ions  move  freely  through  the metal.
C Metals conduct electricity because there is a strong force of attraction between the positive ions and the delocalised electrons.
D Metals have a high melting point because the positive ions attract each other strongly. 4

questions.question: Which statement about fuels is correct? A Coal and ethanol are examples of non-renewable energy sources., B Hydrogen and oxygen can be reacted to produce an electric current., C Large amounts of energy are taken in by a fuel when it burns., D Radioactive isotopes are burned to produce heat.

D Radioactive isotopes are burned to produce heat.

questions.question: Which statement explains why increasing the concentration of a reactant increases the rate of the reaction? A A greater proportion of the particles have the activation energy so there are more successful collisions., B Particles have more energy so there are more frequent collisions., C There are more particles in the same volume so there are more frequent collisions., D The particles move more quickly so there are more frequent collisions.
questions.text: Which statement explains why increasing the concentration of a reactant increases the rate of the reaction? A A greater proportion of the particles have the activation energy so there are more successful collisions., B Particles have more energy so there are more frequent collisions., C There are more particles in the same volume so there are more frequent collisions., D The particles move more quickly so there are more frequent collisions.
options: A A greater proportion of the particles have the activation energy so there are more successful collisions.
B Particles have more energy so there are more frequent collisions.
C There are more particles in the same volume so there are more frequent collisions.
D The particles move more quickly so there are more frequent collisions.

questions.question: Water is added to anhydrous copper( II) sulfate. What happens during the reaction? A The copper( II) sulfate turns blue and the solution formed gets colder., B The copper( II) sulfate turns blue and the solution formed gets hotter., C The copper( II) sulfate turns white and the solution formed gets colder., D The copper( II) sulfate turns white and the solution formed gets hotter.

options: A The copper( II) sulfate turns blue and the solution formed gets colder.
B The copper( II) sulfate turns blue and the solution formed gets hotter.
C The copper( II) sulfate turns white and the solution formed gets colder.
D The copper( II) sulfate turns white and the solution formed gets hotter.

questions.question: Elements in Group IV of the Periodic Table are shown. carbon silicon germanium tin lead What does not occur in Group IV as it is descended? questions.question: Some properties of four elements, P, Q, R and S, are shown. Solid P reacts with dilute hydrochloric acid to give hydrogen. Solid Q does not conduct electricity. Which elements are metals? A P and R B   P and S C   Q and R D   Q and S

questions.question: A magnesium block is attached to iron to prevent it from rusting. Which statement about this method of rust prevention is correct? A Magnesium corrodes instead of iron because it is more reactive., B Magnesium prevents oxygen from reaching the iron., C The iron does not rust because it has a greater tendency to form ions than magnesium., D This method of rust prevention is called galvanising. 12

B Magnesium prevents oxygen from reaching the iron.
C The iron does not rust because it has a greater tendency to form ions than magnesium.
D This method of rust prevention is called galvanising. 12

A 1 and 2 B   1 and 3 C 2 only D 3 only 14
questions.text: Butene has three structural isomers which are alkenes. Which statements about these isomers are correct? 1   They have the same molecular formula. 2   They have different numbers of bonds in the molecule. 3   They have a C=C bond in the structure. A 1 and 2 B   1 and 3 C 2 only D 3 only 14
options: A 1 and 2 B   1 and 3 C 2 only D 3 only 14

questions.question: The hydrocarbon C 12 H 26 is cracked to give X and Y, as shown. C 12 H 26 ’é«  X  +  Y Which statement is correct? A If X is C 6 H 12 then Y will react with aqueous bromine., B If X is C 10 H 22 then Y can be used to make a polymer., C If X is a hydrogen molecule then Y is an alkane., D X and Y could be structural isomers.
questions.text: The hydrocarbon C 12 H 26 is cracked to give X and Y, as shown. C 12 H 26 ’é«  X  +  Y Which statement is correct? A If X is C 6 H 12 then Y will react with aqueous bromine., B If X is C 10 H 22 then Y can be used to make a polymer., C If X is a hydrogen molecule then Y is an alkane., D X and Y could be structural isomers.
options: A If X is C 6 H 12 then Y will react with aqueous bromine.
B If X is C 10 H 22 then Y can be used to make a polymer.
C If X is a hydrogen molecule then Y is an alkane.
D X and Y could be structural isomers.

questions.question: An ester, C 4 H 8 O 2 , is made by reacting 0.06 mol of ethanol, C 2 H 6 O, and 0.05 mol of ethanoic acid, C 2 H 4 O 2 . C 2 H 6 O  +  C 2 H 4 O 2 ’é«  C 4 H 8 O 2 +  H 2 O 0.0375 mol of the ester was made. What is the percentage yield and the M r of the ester? percentage yield / % M r A 62.5 48, B 75.0 48, C 62.5 88, D 75.0 88
